name: Avery Maintainer
subject: Jungle Grid and agent-runtime
body:
Hi Avery,

I read the public documentation for agent-runtime and noticed Agent Runtime dispatches long-running tool jobs to isolated workers and preserves logs, retries, and artifacts. I’m building Jungle Grid, an execution layer for agent-triggered inference, batch jobs, logs, retries, and artifacts.

The workload you describe seems relevant because teams need reliable compute beyond lightweight tool calls. I thought this might be useful as you develop the project soon: https://junglegrid.dev

Benedict
personalization_claims: Agent Runtime dispatches long-running tool jobs to isolated workers and preserves logs, retries, and artifacts

name: Morgan Builder
subject: Jungle Grid and mcp-worker
body:
Hi Morgan,

I read the public documentation for mcp-worker and noticed MCP Worker lets agent clients launch observable batch inference tasks and retrieve generated files. I’m building Jungle Grid, an execution layer for agent-triggered inference, batch jobs, logs, retries, and artifacts.

The workload you describe seems relevant because teams need reliable compute beyond lightweight tool calls. I thought this might be useful as you develop the project soon: https://junglegrid.dev

Benedict
personalization_claims: MCP Worker lets agent clients launch observable batch inference tasks and retrieve generated files
